items.front: Who decides whether a radar approach and landing are authorized under the weather minima?
items.back: The pilot remains responsible for determining whether the approach and landing are authorized under the applicable weather minima.

items.front: What core information must be issued before a radar approach?
items.back: Issue the altimeter setting and the other listed approach-information items, including applicable weather, before the radar approach begins.

items.front: What turn instruction is required on a no-gyro approach after turn-on to final and before the gate?
items.back: Instruct the aircraft to make half-standard rate turns.

items.front: When should lost-communications instructions be issued for a radar approach likely to encounter IFR weather?
items.back: Issue them as soon as possible after radar identification and radio communications are established, subject to the repeated-approach exception.

items.front: What if radar contact is lost before final approach starts?
items.back: Clear the aircraft to an appropriate NAVAID or fix for an instrument approach.

items.front: When is the landing check advisory issued on a normal radar-approach pattern?
items.back: Issue it on downwind in time for the pilot to complete it before turning base.

items.front: How often must an aircraft receive position information before final approach begins?
items.back: At least once before final approach starts.

items.front: What must be included when changing an aircraft to the final controller frequency?
items.back: Include the name of the facility with the frequency change instruction.

items.front: What is asked on initial contact with the final controller?
items.back: Request a communications check from the aircraft.

items.front: What transmission-acknowledgment instruction is used after contact with the final controller is established?
items.back: Tell the aircraft, while on the final approach course, not to acknowledge further transmissions.

items.front: When must a specific missed-approach procedure be issued on a radar approach?
items.back: Before final descent for a full-stop landing whenever any part of final approach will be conducted in IFR conditions.

items.front: When are climb-out or departure instructions issued for a low approach or touch-and-go?
items.back: Issue them before final descent begins, and include a specific heading and altitude unless the aircraft will maintain VFR and contact the tower.

items.front: What must the final controller obtain from the tower for an aircraft on final to a towered airport?
items.back: Obtain the landing, touch-and-go, or low-approach clearance, then pass that clearance and the surface wind to the aircraft.

items.front: What if a safe radar approach becomes questionable because of final-approach abnormalities?
items.back: If the runway environment is not in sight, instruct the aircraft to execute the missed approach already issued or climb to a specified altitude and fly a specified course.

items.front: When may a military single-frequency approach aircraft be required to change frequency after the approach begins?
items.back: Only for the listed exceptions, such as completion of landing or low approach, daylight VFR, pilot request, emergency, visual approach, or IFR cancellation.
